summary: Implemented job status display in Chat view with /status and /inspect commands
reasoning: Created job status display infrastructure per specs/tui.md section 4 and specs/orchestration.md section 7. Key decisions: (1) Added job state tracking to Chat view: job_active flag, job_phase, job_start_time, job_cost, job_cost_ceiling, job_leads map (tracking per-Lead status), job_lead_count, job_completed_count; (2) Implemented event handlers for job events: :job_started (initializes job state with cost ceiling and lead count), :job_phase_change (updates current phase), :lead_started (adds Lead to job_leads map with deliverable and status), :lead_completed (marks Lead as complete, increments completed count), :lead_status_change (updates Lead status to :running/:waiting/:blocked/:complete), :job_cost_update (updates job cost), :job_completed (resets job state); (3) Modified status bar rendering to conditionally show job info when job_active is true: format_job_status shows 'N leads │ X/N complete │ $X.XX/$Y.YY │ Zm elapsed │ ◉ phase' matching spec format exactly; (4) Implemented format_elapsed_time helper with three tiers: <60s shows seconds, <60m shows minutes, >=60m shows hours+minutes; (5) Implemented /status command: shows detailed job status including phase, leads (complete/total), cost (current/ceiling), elapsed time, and per-Lead status list with deliverable names; shows 'No active job' when not in orchestration mode; (6) Implemented /inspect <lead_id> command: parses --last N and --type TYPE options, validates Lead exists in current job, shows placeholder message for site log entries (actual site log reading will be implemented with Deft.Store integration once orchestration is complete); shows helpful error messages for invalid usage, non-existent Leads, or when no job is active; (7) Lead status display uses icons: ◉ (running), ○ (waiting), ⊗ (blocked), ✓ (complete); (8) Job phase formatting: :planning, :researching, :decomposing, :executing, :verifying show as '◉ phase', :complete shows as '✓ complete'; (9) Implementation is forward-compatible: TUI is ready to receive and display job events once orchestration GenServers are implemented; job event format matches orchestration spec section 6 message protocol; (10) All handlers are placeholder-safe: job events are caught with catch-all handler that ignores unknown events, preventing crashes if orchestration sends additional event types; (11) Status bar switches seamlessly between session mode (tokens, memory, cost, turn count) and job mode (leads, cost ceiling, elapsed time) based on job_active flag; (12) /inspect command provides helpful feedback: lists available Leads when invalid Lead ID is provided, shows usage message on incorrect syntax; (13) All code passes format and credo checks, all tests pass (392 tests, 0 failures)

summary: Implemented SessionPicker Breeze view for session resume
reasoning: Created lib/deft/tui/session_picker.ex to implement session picker view per specs/tui.md section 7. Key decisions: (1) Used Breeze.View with mount/2, render/1, and handle_event/3 following pattern from existing Chat view; (2) mount/2 calls Deft.Session.Store.list/0 to load all sessions - list/0 returns sorted sessions (most-recent-first) with metadata including session_id, working_dir, created_at, last_message_at, message_count, last_user_prompt; (3) State includes sessions list, selected_index (starting at 0), and optional on_select callback function; (4) render/1 displays sessions in a list format using render_session/3 helper - shows session_id (first 8 chars), working_dir (abbreviated with ~ for home, truncated to 30 chars), relative timestamp (Ns/Nm/Nh/Nd ago or YYYY-MM-DD for old sessions), message count, and last user prompt (truncated to 40 chars); (5) Selected session is highlighted with 'bold reverse' style, unselected sessions use normal style; (6) handle_event/3 implements keyboard navigation: Up arrow calls handle_navigation(:up) to decrement selected_index (clamped to 0), Down arrow calls handle_navigation(:down) to increment selected_index (clamped to length-1), Enter calls handle_select/0 to invoke optional on_select callback and send {:session_selected, session_id} message before stopping view, q/Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D stop the view without resuming; (7) Display handles empty state gracefully - shows 'No previous sessions found' message when Store.list/0 returns empty list or error, hides keyboard shortcuts except quit when no sessions; (8) Session line format optimized for readability: '[session_id] working_dir | timestamp | N msgs | last prompt' - session_id abbreviated to 8 chars for compactness, working_dir and prompt truncated with ellipsis if too long; (9) Timestamp formatting uses relative time for recent sessions (seconds/minutes/hours/days ago) and absolute date (YYYY-MM-DD) for sessions older than 7 days - improves UX by making recency immediately visible; (10) Implementation is pure view logic - session resume orchestration (what happens when user selects a session) is left to the caller that mounts this view, enabling flexible integration

summary: Implemented slash command dispatch with parse, route, and error handling
reasoning: Completed slash command dispatch per specs/tui.md section 6 by integrating existing Deft.SlashCommand module into TUI chat view. Key decisions: (1) Replaced TODO comment in handle_user_input/1 with call to new handle_slash_command/2 helper function; (2) handle_slash_command/2 uses SlashCommand.parse/1 to recognize leading '/' and extract command name + args; (3) For parsed commands, calls SlashCommand.dispatch/1 to look up command/skill in Registry and load definition; (4) Command dispatch returns {:ok, :command, definition} or {:ok, :skill, definition} or error tuples; (5) Commands (type :command) are injected as user messages by returning {:submit, text} - combines definition with args if present; (6) Skills (type :skill) are also submitted for now with TODO comment noting proper skill injection requires agent skill support (future work); (7) Error handling: {:error, :not_found, name} shows 'Unknown command: /name' system message, {:error, :no_definition, name} shows 'Command /name exists but has no definition' system message; (8) Both error cases return {:command_handled, new_term} to prevent passing invalid commands to agent; (9) Added alias for Deft.SlashCommand at module top per Credo suggestion (avoids nested module references); (10) Three TUI-owned commands (/help, /clear, /quit) continue to be handled directly before dispatch - no changes to existing implementation; (11) Dispatch mechanism enables other specs to own their commands: /model and /cost route to sessions spec, /compact routes to harness spec, /observations, /forget, /correct route to observational-memory spec, /status, /inspect, /plan route to orchestration spec; (12) Implementation is forward-compatible: as other specs implement their command handlers and register them in Skills Registry, slash command dispatch will automatically route to them with no TUI code changes needed

summary: Implemented multi-line input support with three input methods
reasoning:
Implemented user input component per specs/tui.md section 5 with three methods for inserting newlines: (1) Shift+Enter (Kitty protocol) - added handle_event clause matching keys 'shift-enter', 'S-enter', 'Shift-Enter' that appends '
' to input without submitting; (2) Backslash+Enter fallback - modified Enter handler to check String.ends_with?(input, ''), removes trailing backslash and appends '
' instead of submitting; (3) Paste detection - added last_char_timestamp state field to track System.monotonic_time(:millisecond) of each character; characters arriving within 5ms are detected as paste via is_paste_event?/2 helper; pasted text preserves embedded newlines via should_append_character?/2 which accepts String.printable?(key) or paste-with-newlines; (4) Refactored character input handling to reduce cyclomatic complexity - extracted handle_character_input/2, is_paste_event?/2, and should_append_character?/2 helper functions to keep Credo complexity under threshold; (5) Updated help text to document Shift+Enter and backslash+Enter shortcuts; (6) Input history (Up/Down arrow) was already implemented correctly - no changes needed; (7) All paste detection logic uses millisecond timestamps and 5ms threshold per spec requirement 'paste detection (chars within 5ms = literal newlines)'

(9) extract_key_arg/2 extracts display argument per tool: file_path for read/write/edit, command for bash, pattern for grep, path for ls/find, key for cache_read; (10) Status bar formatting: format_tokens shows current/window as '12.4k/200k', format_memory shows tokens/threshold or '--' before first observation, format_cost shows '$0.42', agent state shows filled (◉) or empty (○) circle with state name; (13) Input handling: Enter submits prompt via Deft.Agent.prompt/2, Up/Down navigate input history, backspace deletes chars, printable chars append to input buffer, Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D quit; (14) Implemented /help, /clear, /quit commands directly in TUI per spec section 6 - other slash commands TODO for future work items; (15) Uses assign/2 in render/1 to set model_name and agent_state_display in assigns (HEEx template variables requirement); (16) commit_streaming_message/1 helper creates assistant message from accumulated current_text when transitioning to :idle, resets streaming state and clears active_tools; (17) calculate_cost/3 helper estimates cost using per-model pricing (Opus $15/$75, Haiku $0.25/$1.25, Sonnet $3/$15 per MTok); (18) All code passes format and credo checks with cyclomatic complexity under threshold

Key decisions: (1) Built POC simulating 1200 lines of mixed content (markdown, code blocks, lists, plain text) to exceed 1000+ line requirement; (2) Simulated 30 tokens/sec streaming rate by scheduling :stream_token messages every 33ms; (3) Used Breeze.View API with mount/2, render/1, handle_event/3, handle_info/2 following Breeze patterns from deps; (4) Implemented three-section layout: scrollable content area (height-30), fixed input bar, fixed status bar showing tokens, lines, and streaming state; (5) Created both interactive (Breeze.Server.start_link) and fallback (non-interactive) test modes; (6) Fallback test results: 1200 lines, 18,323 tokens, 41 seconds elapsed, 446 tokens/sec actual rate, 49 MB memory usage - all well within acceptable limits; (7) Performance validation: Total time 41s < 60s threshold (✅), sustained 446 tokens/sec > 30 tokens/sec minimum (15x faster, ✅), memory 49 MB reasonable (✅); (8) Documented findings in docs/breeze_performance_report.md with recommendation to proceed with Breeze for production TUI (no need for Termite + BackBreeze fallback); (9) Removed @impl attributes since Breeze.View doesn't define formal behavior; (10) POC validates Breeze can handle streaming LLM output with significant performance headroom

summary: Implemented cache_read tool for retrieving cached tool results
reasoning: Created lib/deft/tools/cache_read.ex implementing cache_read tool per specs/filesystem.md section 6.3. Key decisions: (1) Extended Deft.Tool.Context with optional cache_tid field to provide cache access to tools - cache_tid is the ETS table reference for direct reads without GenServer calls; (2) Tool only included in agent tool list when session has active cache entries per spec requirement; (3) Implemented three parameter types: key (required), lines (optional line range like '740-760'), filter (optional regex pattern); (4) Line filtering uses 1-indexed inclusive ranges matching spec examples, extracts via Enum.slice after parsing START-END format; (5) Pattern filtering uses Regex.compile and filters lines matching pattern, returns '(no matches for pattern: X)' message when no matches found; (6) Combined filtering applies line range first then pattern filter to the subset; (7) Error handling: returns ':miss' message when key not found in cache, 'Cache not available' when cache_tid is nil (session ended), validates line range format; (8) Data type handling: implemented to_result_string/1 helper that uses inspect/1 for non-string types (maps, lists) to get readable output - to_string/1 doesn't implement String.Chars protocol for these types; (9) Store.read already has try/rescue for ArgumentError returning :miss when table owner crashes, so cache expiration (nil cache_tid) is the main error case; (10) Created comprehensive test suite test/deft/tools/cache_read_test.exs with 22 tests covering all parameter combinations, filtering modes, error cases, and data types; (11) All tests pass, code passes format/credo checks

The Issues.close/2 function was already implemented in the GenServer. All tests pass (348 tests, 0 failures), formatter and linter clean.

decision: Implemented `deft issue update <id>` CLI command with --title, --priority, --status, and --blocked-by flags. The --blocked-by flag accepts a comma-separated list of issue IDs and replaces (not appends to) the entire dependencies list, consistent with the update/2 API design.
rationale: Replace semantics for --blocked-by flag aligns with how Issues.update/2 handles the dependencies field - it replaces the entire list rather than appending. This is simpler and more predictable than merge semantics.

summary: Added .gitattributes merge=union setup on first issue creation
reasoning: Implemented ensure_gitattributes/1 function called from persist_issues/1 to automatically add '.deft/issues.jsonl merge=union' to .gitattributes on first write. Key decisions: (1) Use git rev-parse --show-toplevel to find repo root; (2) Check if line already exists to avoid duplicates; (3) Handle both existing and new .gitattributes files; (4) Gracefully skip if not in a git repo; (5) Catch and log errors without blocking issue creation; (6) Refactored into three functions (ensure_gitattributes, update_gitattributes_file, append_to_gitattributes_if_needed) to reduce nesting depth and pass credo strict checks. Added comprehensive tests covering: creation of new file, appending to existing file, no duplication, and skipping outside git repos. All tests pass, formatting and linting clean.

summary: Implemented blocked/0 query function
reasoning: Added blocked/0 client API function, handle_call(:blocked) callback, and is_blocked?/2 helper function per spec section 3. Implementation follows the same pattern as ready/0: filters issues where status is :open and at least one dependency has status :open or :in_progress. No sorting needed for blocked issues per spec.

summary: Implemented blocked/0 query function
reasoning: Added blocked/0 client API function, handle_call(:blocked) callback, and is_blocked?/2 helper function per spec section 3. Implementation follows the same pattern as ready/0: filters issues where status is :open and at least one dependency has status :open or :in_progress. No sorting needed for blocked issues per spec.

summary: Improved test coverage for git behavior outside repos
reasoning: The implementation for git behavior outside repos was already complete in Deft.Issues.resolve_file_path/0: when git command fails (not in a git repo), it uses File.cwd!()/.deft/issues.jsonl, and File.mkdir_p! in persist_issues/1 creates the .deft/ directory. However, the existing test 'uses cwd when not in a git repository' was passing an explicit file_path to start_link, which bypassed the automatic resolution logic. Improved the test to use File.cd!/2 to temporarily change working directory, then start Issues without explicit file_path, properly exercising and verifying the automatic path resolution. Test cannot run async due to File.cd affecting entire VM. All tests pass, no formatting or linting issues.

summary: Implemented worktree awareness with Deft.Git behaviour for testability
reasoning: Created Deft.Git behaviour with cmd/1 callback for abstracting git operations, enabling testability through dependency injection. Implemented Deft.Git.System as default adapter using System.cmd/3. Updated Deft.Issues.resolve_file_path/0 to use Deft.Git.cmd/1 instead of direct System.cmd call. Created Deft.GitMock test implementation using Application config for cross-process state sharing (Process dictionary doesn't work across GenServer boundaries). Added comprehensive worktree awareness tests: (1) resolves to main repo when in worktree; (2) uses cwd when not in git repo; (3) handles regular .git directory. Fixed acquire_lock/2 to handle :enoent from File.stat (lock disappeared during check). Fixed persist_issues/1 to create directory before acquiring lock to prevent :enoent from File.open. All existing tests continue to pass.

summary: Implemented Reflector compression eval test with 20 iterations and 90% pass rate threshold
reasoning: Created test/eval/reflector/compression_test.exs implementing the compression target eval from specs/evals/reflector.md. Key decisions: (1) Test runs 20 iterations of Reflector.run/4 with 40k token fixture; (2) Validates output ≤20k tokens (50% compression); (3) Pass rate threshold 90% (18/20) per spec; (4) Added provider registration in setup; (5) Gracefully skips when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set with clear message; (6) Stores results as JSONL in test/eval/results/ with failure examples; (7) Created 128k char (~32k token) fixture with all 5 observation sections; (8) Tagged :eval and :expensive to exclude from default runs; (9) Uses real LLM calls (not mocked) as this is a behavior eval testing actual compression quality; (10) Matches eval spec requirements for fixture design, result storage, statistical testing. Note: Used 90% pass rate from reflector.md spec (more specific than 80% in general table).

summary: Implemented slash command dispatch for CLI with parse/dispatch pattern
reasoning: Created Deft.SlashCommand module with parse/1 (detects leading / and extracts command name + args) and dispatch/1 (looks up in Registry, loads definition). Integrated into CLI.interactive_loop via handle_user_input/1 helper. Key decisions: (1) Commands inject markdown content as user message; (2) Skills inject definition as user message in CLI (TODO comment notes TUI should inject as system instruction when implemented); (3) Unknown commands report error without crashing; (4) Skills without definition (manifest-only) return helpful error; (5) Args appended to definition for both commands and skills; (6) Created comprehensive test suite (14 tests) covering parse/dispatch/integration scenarios; (7) Used SlashCommand alias in CLI to satisfy credo. This provides the foundation for user-initiated slash command invocation (complementing agent-initiated use_skill tool), with TUI system instruction injection deferred until TUI implementation.

summary: Implemented closed issue compaction on startup
reasoning: Added issues_compaction_days config (default 90), implemented compact_closed_issues/3 to filter old closed issues during init, created write_issues_during_init/2 for atomic file rewrite without locking overhead, added comprehensive tests for compaction scenarios

summary: Added skills/commands listing to system prompt
reasoning: Implemented Registry.list() integration in SystemPrompt.build/1 to assemble Available skills and Available commands sections, enabling agent awareness of available skills for suggestion and auto-invocation

summary: Verified JSONL persistence with advisory lock implementation
reasoning: The advisory locking mechanism was already fully implemented in lib/deft/issues.ex (lines 419-519) as part of the initial Deft.Issues GenServer implementation. The implementation includes: (1) persist_issues/1 that sets up lock parameters (stale threshold 30s, retry 100ms, timeout 10s); (2) with_lock/6 that implements retry logic with jitter; (3) acquire_lock/2 that uses File.open with :exclusive flag for atomic lock creation, writes PID and timestamp as JSON line (for debugging stale locks), detects and removes stale locks older than 30s; (4) write_issues/1 that writes to temp file with unique integer suffix then uses File.rename!/2 for atomic rename. All tests pass. This work item completion documents that the feature is verified working.

summary: Added comprehensive test suite for cycle detection on load
reasoning: Cycle detection was already implemented but untested. Created test/deft/issues_test.exs with 4 test cases: (1) simple two-issue cycle detection and clearing; (2) three-issue cycle (A->B->C->A) detection and clearing; (3) valid dependency chain (no cycle) preservation; (4) self-referential cycle detection. Tests verify warnings are logged and dependencies are cleared. All tests use temporary directories to avoid interfering with actual issue files. Used capture_log to verify Logger.warning output. Tests pass cleanly with no warnings after formatting.

summary: Implemented Deft.Issues GenServer with JSONL storage, dedup-on-read, and cycle detection
reasoning: Created GenServer per spec section 4. Key decisions: (1) Used GenServer with in-memory state (list of Issue structs) instead of ETS for simplicity given small issue count; (2) Implemented load_issues/1 with dedup-on-read using Map accumulator where last occurrence wins; (3) Lines that fail JSON.decode skip with Logger.warning (file not corrupt unless all lines malformed); (4) detect_and_fix_cycles/1 runs after loading in init/1, clears dependencies on affected issues; (5) has_cycle?/3 uses MapSet visited tracker with 100-depth safety limit to prevent infinite recursion; (6) check_cycle/2 validates on create/update before persisting; (7) resolve_file_path/0 uses git rev-parse --git-common-dir for worktree awareness, falls back to cwd if not in repo; (8) Advisory locking via with_lock/6 using File.open exclusive mode, stale threshold 30s, retry with jitter, 10s timeout; (9) Lock file contains PID and timestamp as JSON line for debugging; (10) Atomic writes via temp file then File.rename/2; (11) is_ready?/2 checks status == :open and all dependencies are closed; (12) ready/0 returns issues sorted by priority then created_at (oldest first); (13) Exposed API: create/1, update/2, close/1, get/1, list/1, ready/0; (14) Refactored complex handle_call for update into helper functions (do_update, build_updated_issue, update_with_cycle_check, update_without_cycle_check) to satisfy credo complexity limits (max cyclomatic complexity 9, max nesting depth 3); (15) Used with statement for create to reduce nesting

summary: Implemented project-level re-scan on session start via Registry.rescan_project/1
reasoning: Added rescan_project/1 function to Deft.Skills.Registry per spec section 5. Key decisions: (1) Function removes all existing project-level entries from registry and replaces with freshly discovered entries from .deft/ subdirectory; (2) Uses Agent.update/2 to atomically filter out project entries and merge in new discoveries; (3) Called from Session.Supervisor.start_session/1 before starting session worker; (4) Added project_dir parameter to start_session/1 options (defaults to File.cwd!/0); (5) Fixed existing bug where discover_all was looking for project skills in <project_dir>/skills/ instead of <project_dir>/.deft/skills/ - spec clearly states project skills should be in .deft/ subdirectory; (6) Updated both discover_all and rescan_project to use Path.join(project_dir, ".deft") before calling discover_level; (7) Added comprehensive test suite covering: skill/command updates, additions, removals; preservation of non-project entries during rescan; handling of empty project directories; (8) Tests create temporary directories with .deft/skills/ and .deft/commands/ fixtures to verify correct discovery behavior

summary: Wired OM into Agent: Context.build calls State.get_context, injects observations, trims messages; messages_added called after each message addition
reasoning: Integrated observational memory into agent loop per spec section 3. Key decisions: (1) Context.build/2 now accepts session_id parameter and calls OM.State.get_context/1 to get observations and observed message IDs; (2) Used OM.Context.inject/2 to inject observations and trim observed messages; (3) Added defensive checks using Registry.lookup to verify OM.State process exists before calling it - allows agent to work without OM initialized (important for tests); (4) Called notify_om_messages_added/2 after each discrete message addition: user messages (line 194), assistant messages (line 765), tool result messages (line 1124), queued user messages (line 921), and compaction summary messages (line 1383); (5) Used try/catch for get_context calls to handle EXIT signals from GenServer.call when process doesn't exist; (6) Added OMState and OMContext aliases to satisfy credo linter; (7) Calibration factor defaults to 4.0 (TODO: retrieve from OM.State in future work)

summary: Implemented Deft.Skills.Registry as Agent with three-level cascade discovery
reasoning:
Created Agent-based registry that scans built-in (priv/skills, priv/commands), global (~/.deft/skills, ~/.deft/commands), and project (.deft/skills, .deft/commands) directories on init. Key decisions: (1) Used String.split(content, "
---
", parts: 2) to parse YAML manifests, avoiding YamlElixir.read_all_from_string per spec; (2) Files missing --- separator are manifest-only (cannot be invoked); (3) Applied cascade with project > global > builtin priority, skills win over commands at same level; (4) Registry holds map of name → Entry struct; (5) Progressive loading via load_definition/1 uses Agent.get_and_update/2 for atomic read-and-cache; (6) Added error handling with try/catch in discover_all to ensure registry never crashes during init (returns empty map on error); (7) Gracefully handles missing directories (treated as empty, not errors); (8) Validates skill/command names match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ pattern, skips invalid names with warnings; (9) Added to Application supervision tree; (10) Created minimal test suite that works with global registry without interfering with Application startup

summary: Implemented OM resume from snapshot with pending_message_tokens recomputation
reasoning: Added resume functionality per spec section 9.3. Key decisions: (1) Modified State.start_link/1 to accept optional :messages and :snapshot parameters; (2) Modified init/1 to call restore_from_snapshot/3 when snapshot is provided; (3) Implemented restore_from_snapshot/3 that restores all persisted fields from snapshot (active_observations, observation_tokens, observed_message_ids, generation_count, last_observed_at, activation_epoch, calibration_factor), then recomputes pending_message_tokens from messages not in observed_message_ids using Tokens.estimate/2; (4) After restoration, calls check_and_spawn_observer/1 and check_and_spawn_reflector/1 to trigger observation/reflection if thresholds already exceeded; (5) Added load_latest_snapshot/1 public API that reads from separate _om.jsonl file, parses all lines, returns latest entry (or nil if file doesn't exist); (6) Updated OM.Supervisor to accept and pass :messages and :snapshot to State; (7) Updated Session.Worker to accept :om_snapshot and pass to OM.Supervisor; (8) Updated Session.Supervisor.start_session/1 to accept :om_snapshot option; (9) Updated Store.resume/1 to load OM snapshot from separate _om.jsonl file via State.load_latest_snapshot/1 and return as :om_snapshot (replacing old :om_state from main JSONL); (10) Added comprehensive test suite in state_resume_test.exs covering snapshot restoration, pending token recomputation, fresh start, and snapshot file loading. The separate _om.jsonl file approach (from decision #9) avoids JSONL write interleaving between main session log and OM snapshots.

summary: Emit OM events from State module rather than Observer/Reflector functions
reasoning: Observer.run and Reflector.run are pure functions spawned as Tasks without session_id context. Broadcasting requires session_id for Registry dispatch. Emitting events from State module before/after Task spawning provides cleaner separation and keeps Observer/Reflector as pure extraction/compression functions.

summary: Made parse_sections/1 public in Observer.Parse
reasoning: Hard cap implementation needs to parse observation sections. Rather than duplicate the logic, made the existing private function public for reuse.

summary: Implemented circuit breaker for OM cycle failures
reasoning: Added circuit breaker functionality to Deft.OM.State per spec section 6.3. Key decisions: (1) Added three new state fields: consecutive_failures (integer, tracks cycle failures), circuit_open (boolean, degraded mode flag), circuit_opened_at (DateTime, for cooldown tracking). (2) Implemented record_cycle_success/1 helper that resets consecutive_failures to 0 on successful Observer/Reflector completion - called in handle_info for both observer_ref and reflector_ref success cases. (3) Implemented record_cycle_failure/2 helper that increments consecutive_failures, emits {:om, :cycle_failed, %{type: type, reason: reason}} via broadcast_event/2, checks if circuit should open (>= 3 failures), calls open_circuit/1 if threshold reached - called in handle_info for both observer_ref and reflector_ref :DOWN cases. (4) Added open_circuit/1 helper that sets circuit_open: true, circuit_opened_at: DateTime.utc_now(), logs warning, and emits {:om, :circuit_open} event via broadcast_event/2. (5) Implemented can_attempt_cycle?/1 guard that checks circuit_open flag and calculates cooldown (5 minutes = 300 seconds) - returns true if circuit closed OR if circuit open but cooldown expired. (6) Implemented reset_circuit/1 helper that clears circuit_open, circuit_opened_at, and consecutive_failures - called after successful cooldown in spawn_observer_task/2 and spawn_reflector_task/1. (7) Added broadcast_event/2 helper using Registry.dispatch(Deft.Registry, {:session, session_id}, ...) pattern matching agent.ex - events sent as {:om_event, event} for TUI consumption. (8) Modified spawn_observer_task/2 and spawn_reflector_task/1 to check can_attempt_cycle?/1 before spawning - if circuit open, log debug message and return state unchanged. (9) Modified Observer success handler to check if result.observations is empty (indicates retry failure) and record as failure - prevents treating failed retries as success. Circuit breaker prevents runaway LLM costs during persistent failures and allows automatic recovery after cooldown or explicit /compact command (future work).

summary: Implemented sync fallback for force_observe with 1 retry max
reasoning: Added handle_call for :force_observe that implements the from-stashing pattern per spec section 6.3. Key decisions: (1) On :force_observe call, stash the caller's from in sync_from field, spawn Observer Task with retry logic, return {:noreply, state} to avoid blocking. (2) Added run_observer_with_retry/5 and run_observer_with_retry_loop/6 functions that wrap Observer.run with retry logic - retries up to max_retries times (1 for sync path) with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s...). Empty observations indicates failure. (3) Modified handle_info for Observer Task completion ({ref, result}) to check if sync_from is set - if so, reply via GenServer.reply(sync_from, {:ok, result}) and clear sync_from, otherwise continue with normal async buffering path. (4) Modified handle_info for Observer Task DOWN ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) to check if sync_from is set - if so, reply via GenServer.reply(sync_from, {:error, reason}) and clear sync_from, otherwise continue with normal async error handling. (5) Added early return in force_observe if no unobserved messages exist. The 60s timeout is enforced by the caller (Agent would call GenServer.call(State, :force_observe, 60_000)). This prevents deadlock while allowing State to remain responsive to other calls during the sync observation.

summary: Implemented Observer/Reflector serialization for concurrent coordination
reasoning: Added serialization logic to ensure Observer and Reflector tasks do not run concurrently per spec section 6.4. Key decisions: (1) In check_and_spawn_observer/1, added check for not state.is_reflecting before activating buffered chunks - if Reflector is running, Observer results remain buffered until reflection completes. (2) After Observer completes (handle_info for observer_ref), added call to check_and_spawn_reflector/1 to trigger reflection now that is_observing is false. (3) After Reflector completes (handle_info for reflector_ref), added check for buffered chunks - if chunks exist and pending_message_tokens >= threshold, activate them now that is_reflecting is false. (4) Refactored check_and_spawn_observer/1 to reduce cyclomatic complexity from 10 to acceptable level by extracting two helpers: maybe_activate_buffered_chunks/1 (checks threshold + chunks + not reflecting, calls activate_buffered_chunks if true) and maybe_spawn_observer_for_buffering/3 (handles observer spawning logic with buffer intervals). (5) activation_epoch increments were already in place (activate_buffered_chunks line 393, Reflector completion line 242) per spec requirement. (6) The serialization rule is bidirectional: check_and_spawn_reflector/1 already checks not state.is_observing before spawning Reflector (line 404), and now check_and_spawn_observer checks not state.is_reflecting before activating chunks. This prevents race conditions where Reflector replaces active_observations while Observer results are computed against stale pre-reflection observations. Both processes can buffer work in parallel, but activation is serialized.

summary: Implemented Deft.Issue.Id.generate/1 for unique hash-based IDs
reasoning: Created Deft.Issue.Id module per spec section 2.1 (ID Generation). Key decisions: (1) Used :crypto.strong_rand_bytes(16) instead of UUID library since no UUID dependency exists in mix.exs - provides cryptographically strong random data. (2) Converted random bytes to lowercase hex string using Base.encode16(case: :lower) for consistent formatting. (3) Implemented recursive collision detection via find_unique_id/3 helper that starts with 4 hex characters and extends to 5, 6, etc. on collision. (4) Used simple linear search (candidate_id in existing_ids) for collision detection - acceptable for small issue tracker scale (tens to hundreds of issues). (5) Created comprehensive test suite with 11 test cases covering ID format validation, uniqueness, collision handling, and edge cases. Module provides ID generation for issue creation - GenServer and persistence will be implemented in subsequent work items.

summary: Defined Deft.Issue struct with JSON encode/decode support
reasoning: Created Deft.Issue struct module per spec section 2 (Issue Schema). Key decisions: (1) Used @derive Jason.Encoder for automatic JSON encoding following existing patterns in session/entry.ex. (2) Implemented encode/1 wrapper that returns {:ok, json} | {:error, reason} tuple for consistent error handling. (3) Implemented decode/1 that uses Jason.decode with keys: :atoms option matching patterns in session/store.ex and other JSONL-handling code. (4) Added from_map/1 helper to convert decoded JSON maps to Issue structs with proper type coercion. (5) Implemented normalize_status/1 and normalize_source/1 private functions to handle both atom and string representations of status (:open | :in_progress | :closed) and source (:user | :agent) fields - this enables robust decoding from JSON where these are serialized as strings. (6) Added timestamp/0 helper function that returns DateTime.utc_now() |> DateTime.to_iso8601() per spec section 2 requirement for consistent ISO 8601 UTC timestamp format across all Issue fields. (7) Used @enforce_keys for all required fields except closed_at and job_id which are explicitly nullable per spec section 2. (8) Included comprehensive moduledoc and function docs with examples for encode/decode/from_map/timestamp functions. Module provides foundational data structure for issue tracking - GenServer, persistence, and CLI commands will be implemented in subsequent work items.

summary: Implemented Reflector prompt with configurable compression levels
reasoning: Created Deft.OM.Reflector.Prompt module per spec section 4. Implemented system/1 function that takes :target_size (default 20k tokens) and :compression_level (0-3) parameters. Key decisions: (1) Created separate compression_level_guidance/1 helper that returns level-specific instructions (Level 0: gentle/judgmental, Level 1: moderate with 1-day retention, Level 2: aggressive dropping all 🟢 items, Level 3: maximum compression keeping only 🔴 and most recent day). (2) Included section ordering constraint with MUST PRESERVE THIS ORDER emphasis (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) per spec section 4.4. (3) Added per-section token budget guidance per spec section 4.4: Current State ~500, User Preferences ~1k, Files & Architecture ~8k, Decisions ~3k, Session History remainder. (4) Emphasized CORRECTION marker preservation with CRITICAL label and explicit instructions to keep verbatim at all costs per spec section 4.4. (5) Implemented format_number/1 helper to format target_size with thousands separator for readability (20000 → "20,000"). (6) Structured prompt with clear sections: Compression Task (target + level guidance), Section Structure (ordering + budgets), CORRECTION Markers (preservation rules), Compression Strategies (merge/drop/preserve patterns), What NOT to Do (constraints), Output Format (markdown structure). Module provides system prompt generation for Reflector LLM calls - actual Task execution will be implemented in separate work item.

summary: Implemented observation activation with section-aware merge
reasoning: Added activation logic to State.check_and_spawn_observer/1 that triggers when pending_message_tokens >= 30k AND buffered_chunks is non-empty. Key decisions: (1) Activation happens before spawning new observer tasks - this ensures buffered chunks are merged before starting new buffer cycles. (2) Used Deft.OM.Observer.Parse.merge_observations/2 to perform section-aware merge - Current State section is replaced, User Preferences/Decisions/Session History are appended, Files & Architecture uses append-with-dedup. (3) All message_ids from all chunks are collected and added to observed_message_ids. (4) Pending tokens are reduced by the sum of all chunk message_tokens (the raw token count that was compressed). (5) activation_epoch is incremented and snapshot_dirty is set to true. (6) last_observed_at is set to DateTime.utc_now(). (7) Fixed bug in State and Supervisor via_tuple functions - they were using Deft.Registry (duplicate keys for pub/sub) instead of Deft.ProcessRegistry (unique keys for :via tuples), causing "via is not supported for duplicate registries" error. Created comprehensive test suite with 6 test cases covering activation logic, section-aware merge behavior, and edge cases (no activation when pending < threshold, no activation when chunks empty).

summary: Implemented Observer Task execution with actual LLM calls
reasoning: Replaced placeholder Observer Task with full implementation. Key decisions: (1) Created Deft.OM.Observer module with run/4 function that takes Config, messages, existing_observations, and calibration_factor; formats messages using Prompt.format_messages/1, truncates observations to 8k budget using Prompt.truncate_observations/2, builds system+user messages, calls LLM via provider.stream/3, collects response with collect_stream_text/2 helper (120s timeout), parses using Parse.parse_output/1, returns map with observations/message_ids/message_tokens/current_task fields. (2) Used same provider as main agent for Observer since config doesn't have separate om.observer_provider field yet (spec section 8 mentions it but current Config implementation only has om_observer_model) - reasonable default and can be extended later without breaking changes. (3) Modified State struct to include config and messages fields (not just tokens) since Observer needs full message content; updated @enforce_keys and defstruct accordingly. (4) Modified State.messages_added/2 to store full Message structs and calculate tokens using Tokens.estimate/2 on extracted message text (handles all content block types: Text, ToolUse, ToolResult, Thinking, Image). (5) Updated spawn_observer_task/2 to filter unobserved messages (id not in observed_message_ids) and call Observer.run/4 with proper parameters. (6) Threaded config through supervisor chain: Session.Worker passes config to OM.Supervisor, OM.Supervisor passes config to State, State uses config when spawning Observer tasks. (7) Used Task.Supervisor.async_nolink for Observer tasks per spec section 1 (crash isolation). (8) Observer returns empty observations on any failure (parse error, LLM error, provider resolution error) but still tracks message_ids and message_tokens so State knows which messages were attempted. (9) Aliased nested modules (Deft.OM.Observer.{Parse, Prompt}) and Message content block types to satisfy Credo strict mode - no unused aliases.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Observer.Parse module with XML parsing and section-aware merge
reasoning: Created Observer.Parse module per spec sections 3.5 and 3.6. Key decisions: (1) Implemented parse_output/1 to extract <observations> and <current-task> from XML using regex patterns; validates section headers against allowed set (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History); falls back to raw bullet-list extraction when XML tags missing but section headers present; returns error when no structure found. (2) Implemented merge_observations/2 with section-aware merge strategies: Current State uses replace strategy (regenerated each cycle), User Preferences/Decisions/Session History use append strategy, Files & Architecture uses append-with-dedup strategy keyed by filepath (extracts filepaths from 'Read <path>' and 'Modified <path>' patterns, updates existing entries with same path), unknown sections ignored per spec. (3) Implemented parse_sections/1 helper to parse observations into map of section_name => content by scanning for '## ' headers. (4) Implemented extract_filepath/1 helper to extract filepaths from file observation lines using regex matching 'Read' and 'Modified' patterns. Module maintains canonical section ordering (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) in merged output. Created comprehensive test suite with 16 test cases covering all merge strategies and edge cases.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Observer.Prompt module with system prompt and input formatting
reasoning: Created Observer.Prompt module per spec sections 3.3, 3.4, and 3.5. Key decisions: (1) Implemented system/0 function with comprehensive extraction rules covering 🔴 high-priority (user facts, preferences), 🟡 medium-priority (files, errors, commands, architecture, deps, git state, TODOs), and 🟢 low-priority items; included anti-hallucination rules (only extract stated facts, use 'Likely:' prefix for uncertain observations, don't record hypotheticals/explorations); specified sectioned output format (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) with XML wrapping (<observations> and <current-task> tags). (2) Implemented format_messages/1 to convert Deft.Message structs to readable text format with timestamps (HH:MM), role labels, tool call/result annotations ([Tool Call: name], [Tool Result: name]), and image placeholders; skips thinking blocks as they're internal reasoning; truncates very long tool results to ~500 chars for readability. (3) Implemented truncate_observations/2 following spec's 8k budget strategy: takes last 5k tokens (tail), scans remainder for 🔴 high-priority lines filling to 3k budget, prepends '[N observations truncated]' marker if content removed. All functions use Deft.OM.Tokens.estimate/2 with 4.0 calibration factor for consistent token counting. Module provides all input preparation for Observer LLM calls - actual LLM invocation will be implemented in separate Observer.run/2 work item.

summary: Implemented Deft.Store GenServer with ETS+DETS dual registry pattern
reasoning: Implemented Deft.Store GenServer per spec section 2. Key decisions: (1) Added Deft.ProcessRegistry (unique keys) to Application supervisor for :via tuple registration, separate from existing Deft.Registry (duplicate keys for pub/sub events). (2) Used :public ETS table instead of :protected because async load task (separate process) needs write access; access control enforced at GenServer API level via write/4 validation, not ETS level. (3) Separated do_shutdown (flush+close) from do_cleanup (shutdown+delete) so terminate doesn't delete DETS file on normal shutdown - preserves data for restart while cleanup explicitly destroys everything. (4) Registry-based access control for site log uses Registry.lookup(Deft.ProcessRegistry, owner_name) to resolve to PID and compare with caller, survives Foreman process restarts because restarted process re-registers under same name. (5) Async load task spawned with Task.async (linked to GenServer), handles both success ({ref, :loaded}) and failure ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) messages, brutal_kill on cleanup if still running. (6) Cache: lazy flush on buffer size (50 entries) OR timer (5 seconds), no :dets.sync/1 (loss acceptable). Site log: immediate write with :dets.sync/1 on every write (low volume, durability critical). (7) DETS corruption recovery: on :dets.open_file/2 error, delete corrupted file and create new empty file; log warning for site log corruption, silent for cache corruption (ephemeral data).

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.State GenServer with stub Observer Task
reasoning: Implemented the State GenServer per spec sections 1 and 2. Key decisions: (1) Converted State from struct-only module to full GenServer with init/1, handle_call, handle_cast, and handle_info callbacks. (2) Implemented get_context/1 API returning {active_observations, observed_message_ids} tuple as specified in work item. (3) Implemented messages_added/2 API that updates pending_message_tokens and triggers Observer Task spawning at buffer intervals (every 20% of 30k threshold = 6k tokens). (4) Implemented coalescing logic using is_observing and needs_rebuffer flags - if already observing when threshold crossed, sets rebuffer flag instead of spawning duplicate task; when current task completes, checks flag and spawns new task if needed. (5) Added observer_ref and last_buffer_threshold fields to state struct to track in-flight tasks and prevent duplicate spawns. (6) Created stub Observer Task (returns empty observations) since Observer module implementation is a separate work item - infrastructure is complete and will be replaced when Observer.run/2 is implemented. (7) Used Task.Supervisor.async_nolink to spawn Observer tasks for crash isolation per spec section 1. (8) Handled both successful task completion ({ref, result}) and task failure ({:DOWN, ref, ...}) in handle_info with proper cleanup of is_observing flag and observer_ref.

summary: Implemented fixture validation tooling to detect stale fixtures
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.FixtureValidator module and mix eval.validate_fixtures task per spec sections 1.3 and 5. Key decisions: (1) Placed module in lib/deft/eval/ rather than test/eval/support/ so it compiles and is available for mix tasks - validation is a development tool, not test-specific code. (2) Defined @spec_mapping to map fixture directory names to spec names (observer->observational-memory, foreman->orchestration, etc.) for maintainability. (3) Supported both direct spec .md files and subdirectory README.md formats for spec discovery. (4) Excluded hidden fixture files (starting with .) from validation - these are examples/documentation. (5) Extracted version parsing into separate extract_version_from_file/1 function to reduce nesting depth and satisfy Credo strict mode. (6) Added make test.eval.validate_fixtures target for convenient execution. Validation reports total/valid/stale/invalid counts with detailed lists of problematic fixtures showing current vs expected versions.

summary: Created holdout fixture set infrastructure
reasoning: Implemented the holdout fixture set per spec section 1.4. Key decisions: (1) Created fixtures directory structure with holdout subdirectory containing 5 categories (coding_conversations, codebase_snapshots, observation_sets, tool_results, issue_transcripts) mirroring the main fixtures structure. (2) Updated Makefile to add test.eval.holdout target (runs with --only eval --only holdout) and modified test.eval target to exclude holdout tests (--only eval --exclude holdout). (3) Created README.md in both fixtures/ and fixtures/holdout/ documenting the holdout set purpose, usage, and distribution guidelines (20-30% of fixtures reserved for validation). (4) Added .gitkeep files to all empty directories to ensure they're tracked by git. (5) Created example files (.example_fixture.json and .example_test.exs) to demonstrate fixture format and test tagging pattern with @tag :holdout. (6) Results directory already in .gitignore per spec section 2.1. (7) Created support/ directory for future eval helpers. The infrastructure is now in place for developers to add holdout fixtures following the documented guidelines.

summary: Implemented Deft.OM.Supervisor with rest_for_one strategy
reasoning: Implemented the OM supervisor per spec section 1. Key decisions: (1) TaskSupervisor starts first, State second - with rest_for_one strategy, if TaskSupervisor crashes, State also restarts to clear stale is_observing/is_reflecting flags. (2) Both TaskSupervisor and State are registered via Registry with session-scoped names using {:via, Registry, {Deft.Registry, {:om_task_supervisor, session_id}}} pattern for discoverability. (3) Exposed task_supervisor_name/1 as public API so State GenServer (next work item) can reference the TaskSupervisor when spawning Tasks. (4) Passed session_id to State child spec for its own initialization.

summary: Added epoch field to BufferedChunk struct
reasoning: Work item explicitly mentioned epoch field for BufferedChunk. Spec section 6.1 describes epoch-based staleness detection for late-arriving chunks. Added to struct even though not listed in section 2 table.

summary: Implemented comprehensive starvation protection tests using injectable time source
reasoning: Added three test cases to verify starvation protection behavior: (1) 'promotes low-priority requests after 10 seconds' - verifies that a lead request enqueued before a foreman request gets promoted to highest priority after waiting 10s, and is processed first due to earlier enqueue time. (2) 'promotes multiple starved requests in enqueue order' - verifies that multiple requests all waiting >10s are promoted while maintaining their original enqueue order (FIFO among promoted requests). (3) 'does not promote requests that haven't waited long enough' - verifies that requests waiting <10s are not promoted prematurely. Used :atomics for thread-safe time manipulation in tests, allowing precise control over simulated time without actual delays. Tests verify the existing starvation protection implementation works correctly by injecting a custom time source function that reads from the atomics counter.

summary: Implemented priority queue with starvation protection for RateLimiter
reasoning: Added priority queue to RateLimiter GenServer using :gb_trees with keys {-priority, queue_id} for proper ordering. Key decisions: (1) Priority hierarchy: Foreman (3) > Runner (2) > Lead (1) to avoid priority inversion - Runners are spawned by Leads and Leads block on them, so prioritizing Leads over Runners would starve Leads' active work. (2) Used negative priority in queue key to ensure higher numeric priorities dequeue first with :gb_trees.take_smallest. (3) queue_id monotonic counter ensures FIFO within same priority level. (4) Starvation protection via periodic :check_queue timer (1s interval) that scans queue for requests older than 10s and promotes them to highest priority. (5) Queue processing is recursive - after granting permission to one request, immediately tries to process next until buckets exhausted. (6) Reconcile triggers queue processing to unblock waiting requests when capacity becomes available. (7) Injectable time_source in state for testability. (8) Updated API to accept caller_type (:foreman/:runner/:lead) parameter with backward-compatible default of :lead.

summary: Implemented dual token-bucket GenServer with RPM and TPM buckets
reasoning: Created Deft.Job.RateLimiter GenServer following spec section 1. Key decisions: (1) Used hardcoded Anthropic Tier 1 limits (50 RPM, 40k TPM) as defaults since providers.md doesn't define rate limits yet - added TODO to move to configuration when spec is updated. (2) Implemented blocking retry with Process.sleep(100) when buckets insufficient - noted this is temporary until priority queue work item is implemented. (3) Token estimation uses chars/4 heuristic as specified, with support for simple string content, content arrays, and tool_use blocks via JSON encoding. (4) Credit-back after reconciliation is capped at bucket capacity to prevent over-crediting that would allow bursts above configured TPM. (5) Bucket refill uses monotonic time and calculates elapsed seconds to add tokens proportionally - buckets always refill before checking/deducting capacity.

summary: Placed judge calibration module in lib/deft/eval/ rather than test/eval/support/
reasoning: Judge calibration functionality needs to be compiled and available at runtime, not just during test execution. The module provides APIs for loading calibration examples, running calibration, calculating precision/recall metrics, and saving results. Test support code in test/ is not compiled into the application, but calibration can be run as a standalone task via mix tasks or interactive sessions, not just during ExUnit tests. Documentation and example datasets remain in test/eval/support/judge_calibration/ as they are reference materials, not executable code.

summary: Verified Tribunal v1.3.5 provides all required assertions and evaluation capabilities
reasoning: Investigated Tribunal package on hex.pm and GitHub. Confirmed v1.3.5 (released 2026-03-05) provides all required deterministic assertions (contains, regex, is_json, max_tokens), LLM-as-judge assertions (faithful, hallucination, pii via req_llm dependency), and evaluation mode (mix tribunal.eval with threshold support). No gaps identified. Documented findings in docs/spikes/tribunal_verification.md including recommendations to use ~> 1.3 version constraint, include req_llm for LLM-as-judge, and leverage ExUnit integration pattern. Ready for eval infrastructure implementation.

summary: Implemented eval diffing command to compare two runs
reasoning: Created mix eval.compare task that loads two eval run JSONL files and compares them. Key decisions: (1) Read JSONL files directly instead of using ResultStore.load() to handle multiple category results per run file, since the spec indicates one file per run_id but potentially multiple categories. (2) Refactored print functions into smaller helpers to reduce nesting depth for Credo compliance. (3) Added ANSI color coding (green for improvements, red for regressions) and soft floor violation warnings for better visual feedback. (4) Show up to 3 failure examples per category for side-by-side comparison without overwhelming output.

summary: Implemented baselines with history tracking and update logic
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.Baselines module to manage baseline tracking per spec section 2.2. Baselines store baseline rate, soft_floor (baseline - 10pp), and history array per category. Update logic ensures baselines only go up - new results are added to history, but baseline only increases if new rate exceeds current baseline. Soft floor is recalculated on each baseline update. Empty baselines.json created at test/eval/ with structure ready for first eval runs. All 17 tests pass including load/save, baseline-only-increases behavior, soft floor checking, and full integration workflow.

summary: Implemented project directory layout with Deft.Project module
reasoning: Created Deft.Project module to handle project-scoped directory creation under ~/.deft/projects/<path-encoded-repo>/ with sessions/, cache/, and jobs/ subdirectories. Path encoding replaces / with - and strips leading -. Git worktree detection via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' ensures monorepos share a single project directory. Symlinks resolved via Path.expand/1 before encoding. All tests pass including comprehensive coverage of encoding, git root resolution, and directory creation.

summary: Implemented eval result storage with automatic cleanup
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.ResultStore module for storing per-run results as JSONL with all required fields (run_id, commit, timestamp, model, category, pass_rate, iterations, cost_usd, failures). Implemented automatic cleanup to keep only last 30 runs. Created mix eval.export task for archiving results. Added test/eval/results/ to .gitignore.

summary: Major evals spec expansion based on 3-reviewer expert panel
reasoning: Deployed ML/AI eval expert, testing strategy expert, and product/UX eval expert in parallel. Key outcomes: increased iterations from 10 to 20-30, added holdout set requirement, added threshold tuning grid search methodology, moved format assertions from statistical to hard, added end-to-end task battery, added overnight loop safety evals, added bootstrapping strategy for pre-agent evals.

summary: Threshold tuning via grid search, not guessing
reasoning: Per-tool configurable thresholds with defaults determined by grid search over [2k,4k,8k,12k,16k,24k] tokens against 20 realistic tasks. Measure task completion vs context consumption, find the knee. Different tools have different information densities.

summary: Verification circuit breaker is highest-priority eval
reasoning: If the Foreman marks broken work as done, every other eval is suspect. This is the safety net for the entire pipeline. False positives here are the most expensive failure mode. Must be built before end-to-end evals.

summary: Rejected finding: turn counter off-by-one already fixed
reasoning: Agent reported turn_count not incremented on initial call, but specd_history.md shows this was already fixed — check changed from > to >= at line 1140, making total calls equal max_turns exactly.

summary: Rejected finding: async compaction is intentional design
reasoning: Agent reported async compaction doesn't protect triggering call. But async compaction was itself a fix (specd_history.md line 8) to avoid blocking gen_statem. The 70% threshold leaves 30% headroom — the triggering call won't exceed the window.

summary: Marked harness v0.1 as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audit found 0 confirmed findings. Both agent-reported issues were either already fixed (turn counter) or intentional design (async compaction). All spec requirements are met.

summary: ETS+DETS over human-readable files for cache/diary storage
reasoning: Cache and diary are runtime state, not user-facing data. Nobody needs to cat these files. Binary DETS is fine — tools interact through the GenServer API. ETS gives concurrent reads without process bottleneck. DETS handles crash recovery persistence.

summary: Replace Site Log with OTP messages + diary GenServer
reasoning: Site Log was fighting OTP — serializing to JSONL when message passing is the native coordination mechanism. The BEAM provides FIFO ordering, prevents collisions. Diary GenServer replaces data function, direct process messages replace coordination function.

summary: Diary is Foreman-write only, programmatic promotion via pattern matching
reasoning: Foreman has the birds-eye view. Programmatic rules (auto-promote contracts, decisions, corrections; never promote status/blockers) are cheap and predictable. No LLM call needed. Foreman can still write arbitrary entries via its own judgment for edge cases.

summary: Cache and diary are same GenServer module, different instances
reasoning: Same module (Deft.Store), different configs. Access control, write policy, and lifecycle are configuration, not code. One instance for cache (session-scoped, lazy writes, any agent writes), one for diary (job-scoped, sync writes, Foreman-only writes).

summary: Project-scoped directory layout under ~/.deft/projects/
reasoning: Mirrors Claude Code's layout. Each project (git repo) gets its own directory with sessions, cache, jobs. Path-encoded repo name as directory name. Issues stay in project .deft/ (committed to git), runtime state lives in ~/.deft/projects/ (not committed).

summary: Skills are explicit invocation only, no auto-triggering
reasoning: Context-based activation adds complexity and unpredictability. Explicit slash command invocation is simple and predictable. The agent can suggest a skill but cannot invoke one on its own.

summary: Commands are just markdown prompts, skills are structured YAML
reasoning: Two levels of complexity: commands are trivially easy to create (write a markdown file, drop it in directory). Skills are richer (YAML manifest + definition, progressive loading). Both invoked via slash command.

summary: Three-level cascade: built-in → global → project
reasoning: Mirrors config cascade. Users override built-in behavior without forking. Projects specialize without affecting global. Global skills in ~/.deft/skills/, project skills in .deft/skills/.

summary: Moved retry counter reset from :calling → :streaming to successful stream completion
reasoning: The retry counters (retry_count and retry_delay) were being reset at three transition points from :calling to :streaming (lines 285-286, 304-305, 323-324). This broke the retry logic: when a stream partially succeeded then errored, the retry would transition back to :calling, and on the next stream start would reset the counters to 0, allowing unlimited retries instead of the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Fixed by removing resets from those transitions and adding reset to handle_stream_done (when stream successfully completes). Retry counters are now only reset on: (1) successful stream completion, (2) new user prompt, (3) new provider call after tool execution, (4) max retries exceeded — all appropriate locations.

summary: Confirmed finding: retry counter reset on streaming transition allows infinite retries
reasoning: Agent.ex:284-286 resets retry_count to 0 and retry_delay to 1000 on every :calling → :streaming transition. If a stream partially succeeds then errors, handle_stream_error increments retry_count from 0, transitions back to :calling, and the next successful stream start resets it again. This bypasses the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Verified against actual code — the reset at line 285 is unconditional.

summary: Rejected 4 agent findings for harness v0.1
reasoning: (1) Turn limit off-by-one: already fixed per history, code uses >= correctly. (2) Spec missing Deft.Registry in process tree: spec mentions Registry in behaviors section, code correct, cosmetic gap. (3) Project context :system role after messages: Anthropic provider splits system messages via Enum.split_with at line 410, ordering irrelevant. (4) Async compaction: spec doesn't mandate synchronous compaction, async was intentional fix for blocking gen_statem (per history), 70% threshold leaves headroom.

summary: Fixed terminate_child calls to use PID instead of ref
reasoning: Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 requires a child PID, not a reference. The previous implementation at agent.ex:935 and agent.ex:950 was calling it with task.ref (a t:reference/0), which silently returns {:error, :not_found} without actually terminating tasks. Fixed by storing both task.ref and task.pid in task_info maps (for tool tasks) and data.compaction_task_pid (for compaction task), then passing the PID to terminate_child. This ensures abort properly kills in-flight tool and compaction tasks instead of orphaning them. Corrects the erroneous decision from 2026-03-17T03:02:33Z which incorrectly claimed ref was sufficient.

summary: Marked sessions v0.2 as Implemented — no new findings
reasoning: Audit found no new functional bugs. Two agent findings rejected: (1) ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not checked at startup — code defers check to API call time, which is correct behavior for non-API commands like deft config; (2) startup orphan cleanup absent — already tracked in orchestration v0.1 work list and blocked on Draft-status orchestration spec. Remaining work item (interactive mode) is blocked on tui v0.1 Draft, not a code bug.

summary: Added work item: terminate_child called with ref instead of PID
reasoning: cancel_state_operations (agent.ex:935) and cancel_compaction_task (agent.ex:950) pass task.ref to Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2, which requires a PID. The call silently returns {:error, :not_found} and tasks are never terminated during abort. This is distinct from the previously-fixed KeyError crash (specd_history.md) — that crash was fixed by removing .pid access, but the replacement code uses the wrong argument type for terminate_child. Agent finding about turn_count stale in idle was rejected — works correctly since reset happens on next prompt arrival.

summary: Fixed Burrito release config to include wrap step
reasoning: Changed mix.exs:70 from steps: [:assemble] to steps: [:assemble, &Burrito.wrap/1]. This is required for Burrito to produce a self-contained single binary. Without the wrap step, mix release only runs standard Mix release assembly, producing a release directory instead of a portable binary. This implements the spec's distribution requirement (section 6: 'Packaged as a single binary using Burrito from day 1').

summary: Fixed compaction blocking by spawning Task for summarization
reasoning: Moved compaction summarization from synchronous receive loop to async Task spawned under ToolRunner supervisor. Added compaction_task_ref and pending_compaction_data to state. Handle completion via handle_event(:info, {ref, result}) and crash via handle_event(:info, {:DOWN, ref, ...}). Updated all state-specific {:DOWN, ...} handlers to check for compaction task. Extracted abort cancellation logic to reduce cyclomatic complexity. This allows abort, prompts, and DOWN signals to be processed immediately during compaction.

summary: Used Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 for abort handler
reasoning: Fixed abort handler crash by using Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 with task ref instead of Process.exit with pid. This approach is cleaner than storing pid in task_info, as Task.Supervisor properly manages task lifecycle and the ref is sufficient for termination.

summary: Promoted interactive session mode to work item, blocked on tui v0.1 Ready
reasoning: Audit finding recommended Option 2: defer until TUI is Ready. Since tui.md is Draft status, blocking is appropriate. Regular mode using TUI is implied by spec section 5.2 ("In non-interactive mode, no TUI is started"). Created work item with blocking dependency.

summary: Interactive mode stub sent to review — ambiguous TUI dependency
reasoning: deft with no args is a TODO stub (cli.ex:248-253). Spec section 5.1 requires it, but section 5.2 implies regular mode uses TUI (Draft status). Sent to specd_review.md for human decision on whether to implement minimal REPL or defer to TUI spec.

summary: Confirmed Burrito release missing wrap step
reasoning: mix.exs:70 has steps: [:assemble] but Burrito requires steps: [:assemble, &Burrito.wrap/1] per deps/burrito/README.md:135. Without wrap step, mix release produces a standard release directory, not a self-contained binary. The distribution feature is non-functional.

summary: Confirmed compaction blocks gen_statem process
reasoning: collect_stream_text_loop/4 at agent.ex:1266 does a synchronous receive loop inside the gen_statem for up to 30 seconds. During compaction, no messages are processed — abort casts, tool task completions, and DOWN signals are all delayed. This violates the spec's abort guarantee ('User can abort at any time').

summary: Confirmed abort handler crashes on task_info.pid KeyError
reasoning: task_info stored at agent.ex:930 as %{ref: task.ref} (ref only). Abort handler at agent.ex:246-248 iterates tool_tasks and accesses task_info.pid, which raises KeyError. Abort in :executing_tools crashes the agent instead of cleanly stopping tools.

summary: Rejected agent findings: turn counter (duplicate), context.build stub (OM Draft)
reasoning: Turn counter off-by-one already in specd_history.md. Context.build observation injection returning nil is expected — OM spec is Draft status, stub is the correct current behavior.

summary: Rejected agent findings: API key check, model_change, OM state, prompt extract, orphan cleanup
reasoning: API key: only relevant path (non-interactive) does check. model_change: no mechanism to trigger mid-session change exists, type is defined. OM state: OM is Draft. extract_last_user_prompt: keys: :atoms produces atom keys matching %{type: "text"} correctly. Orphan cleanup: depends on Draft orchestration spec.

summary: Implemented interactive session picking in deft resume
reasoning: Added numbered prompt using IO.gets/1 to select from listed sessions. On valid selection, delegates to execute_command({:resume_session, id}, flags). User can quit with 'q'. Invalid selections exit with error message. The session_list is stored to enable indexing after display.

summary: Wire bash_timeout config through to bash tool execution
reasoning: The bash_timeout config field was already defined and loaded but never propagated to the bash tool. Added it to agent_config, Tool.Context struct, and used it as the default timeout in bash.ex instead of the hardcoded @default_timeout_ms constant.

summary: Implemented streaming retry via :calling state transition
reasoning: To implement retry logic for streaming-phase errors, chose to transition from :streaming to :calling state rather than adding a separate retry handler in :streaming. This allows reusing the existing :retry_stream handler at agent.ex:375 which rebuilds context and re-calls call_provider_stream. Also updated stream process crash handler to use same retry path. Pattern matches handle_calling_error/2 behavior: reset partial state (current_message, tool_call_buffers), increment retry_count, exponential backoff, max 3 retries.

summary: Confirmed bash_timeout config never applied to bash tool
reasoning: Agent found bash_timeout is loaded into config and displayed by deft config, but never passed to agent_config or Tool.Context. Bash tool uses hardcoded @default_timeout_ms. Verified: cli.ex:429-436 omits bash_timeout from agent_config, tool.ex:60-61 has no bash_timeout field, bash.ex:52 uses args timeout or constant. User-configured bash_timeout is silently ignored.

summary: Confirmed deft resume missing interactive session picking
reasoning: Spec section 5.1 says deft resume should 'List recent sessions and pick one to resume.' Code at cli.ex:162-192 lists sessions then exits with instructions to use deft resume <id>. The 'pick one' interaction is missing entirely. Not blocked on TUI — a simple IO.gets prompt would suffice.

summary: Confirmed streaming-phase errors bypass retry logic
reasoning: Spec requires 'On provider error, retry with exponential backoff up to 3 times.' handle_calling_error/2 implements retry correctly for :calling state. But handle_stream_error/2 (agent.ex:720-738) transitions directly to :idle with no retry. Mid-stream errors from network blips or rate limits are silently dropped.

summary: Rejected: system messages hoisted to API system param
reasoning: Agent claimed format_messages collapsing all system-role messages into the system param loses ordering. Rejected: Anthropic API requires all system content in the system parameter — this is an API constraint, not a code bug. The logical assembly order from the spec is preserved within the system param.

summary: Rejected: turn count off-by-one (already fixed)
reasoning: Agent claimed initial LLM call not counted against turn_count. Already addressed in specd_history.md — fix changed > to >= in the limit check. Duplicate finding.

summary: Rejected: om_enabled missing from agent config
reasoning: Agent claimed om_enabled defaults to false causing incorrect compaction. Rejected: OM is not implemented (build_observation_injection returns nil). Treating OM as disabled is correct current behavior. Compaction should fire.

summary: Skipped: observation conflict resolution rules (duplicate)
reasoning: Already handled in prior audit — promoted to blocked work item under observational-memory v0.1 in specd_work_list.md. Decision in specd_decisions.jsonl dated 2026-03-16T21:02:00Z.

summary: Rejected: startup orphan cleanup missing
reasoning: Spec section 6 requires scanning for deft/job-* branches from crashed jobs. This is an orchestration feature — orchestration spec is Draft status. Can't clean up job artifacts when jobs don't exist yet. Not a sessions finding.

summary: Promoted %Done{} handler finding to work item
reasoning: Audit finding: %Done{} event silently dropped in :calling state due to missing handler clause. Falls through to catch-all. Recovery via :DOWN handler broadcasts misleading error message. Recommendation: add %Done{} handler in :calling that transitions to :idle cleanly (no error broadcast) for edge case where LLM stream completes without content. Low effort, eliminates misleading error message.

summary: Promoted observation conflict resolution rule to blocked work item
reasoning: Sessions spec section 3 item 5 requires conflict resolution rules in system prompt. Review finding: rules not implemented in current system prompt. Decision: defer to OM implementation (blocked item) rather than add rules about nonexistent observations, which would confuse the LLM. Added as blocked work item under observational-memory v0.1.

summary: Marked providers as Implemented — no functional findings
reasoning: Audited providers spec v0.2 against code. Agent reported format_messages return type mismatch and Done event handling. Rejected format_messages finding: the typespec mismatch is not functionally broken (format_messages is called internally by the provider's own stream/3). Done event handling is a harness concern (agent loop), not providers. No confirmed code bugs in provider layer.

summary: Sent observation conflict resolution rule to review — deferred until OM implementation
reasoning: Sessions spec section 3 item 5 requires observation conflict rules in system prompt. Code has generic rules without observation-specific language. Since OM is not implemented, adding rules about nonexistent observations would confuse the LLM. Filed as review item with recommendation to defer to OM implementation.

summary: Confirmed duplicate ToolResult messages on session resume
reasoning: save_tool_results writes Entry.ToolResult entries, then save_unsaved_messages also writes the tool_result user message as Entry.Message. On resume, reconstruct_messages converts both to Deft.Message structs, producing duplicate tool result user messages. This was introduced by the prior fix that added Entry.ToolResult reconstruction support. Filed as work item.

summary: Sent Done-in-calling-state edge case to review
reasoning: Agent reported %Done{} silently dropped in :calling state (empty LLM response). Recovery works via :DOWN handler but broadcasts misleading error message. Edge case is extremely rare and not clearly broken. Filed as review item with recommendation to add clean handler.

summary: Rejected 3 agent findings: turn counter (duplicate), project context order (matches spec), N separate tool results (subsumed)
reasoning: Turn counter off-by-one already in specd_history.md. Project context after conversation history matches spec section 4 exactly — system messages are extracted by format_messages regardless. N separate tool result messages on resume is part of the duplicate ToolResult finding.

summary: Rejected om_enabled finding — correct behavior while OM unimplemented
reasoning: Agent reported om_enabled missing from agent_config so compaction fires incorrectly. Rejected: OM is not implemented, so om_enabled=false (OM disabled) is correct. Compaction should fire when OM is disabled, which it is. When OM is implemented, the config will need updating as part of OM work.

summary: Convert Entry.ToolResult to user messages with ToolResult content blocks
reasoning: Updated reconstruct_messages/1 to filter both Entry.Message and Entry.ToolResult entries. Added entry_to_message/1 clause for Entry.ToolResult that creates user-role Deft.Message with a single ToolResult content block. Message ID uses 'tool_result_#{tool_call_id}' pattern to ensure uniqueness. This maintains Anthropic's turn structure contract: assistant messages with ToolUse blocks are now properly matched with user messages containing corresponding ToolResult blocks when sessions are resumed.

summary: Implemented extended thinking support in API requests
reasoning: Added thinking config parameter support as specified in providers spec v0.2 line 110. The stream/3 function now reads thinking: true and optional thinking_budget (defaults to 4096) from config, passes them to stream_loop, which adds the required Anthropic thinking parameter {type: enabled, budget_tokens: N} to request body. The existing ThinkingDelta parsing code (lines 220-222) is now reachable when API returns thinking content blocks.

summary: Added Usage event handler in :calling state
reasoning: The message_start SSE event emits Usage{input: N, output: 0} before first content arrives. The catch-all handler at agent.ex:347-349 was discarding it. Added explicit Usage handler in :calling state to call handle_usage/2, ensuring input tokens are properly tracked from the start.

summary: Fixed tool execution to use Task.Supervisor.async_nolink instead of spawn_monitor
reasoning: Prior spawn_monitor implementation provided crash isolation but never sent results back to agent — spawned process exited normally after computing results, agent received {:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, :normal} instead of {ref, results}, causing 'Tool execution was interrupted' error. Replaced spawn_monitor with Task.Supervisor.async_nolink under Deft.Agent.ToolRunner supervisor, which provides both crash isolation (async_nolink prevents crash propagation) AND result delivery (sends {ref, results} on completion). Also added fallback handling when ToolRunner supervisor is unavailable (returns error results immediately). Updated handle_tool_execution_complete to skip Process.demonitor when ref is nil.

summary: reconstruct_messages drops Entry.ToolResult — resumed sessions crash
reasoning: Store.reconstruct_messages/1 filters only Entry.Message, discarding Entry.ToolResult entries. Resumed sessions with tool calls have broken turn structure (ToolUse without matching ToolResult), causing Anthropic API errors. Confirmed at store.ex:199-207. Agent research claimed 4 findings; rejected 3 (test isolation issue, om_enabled default correct for now, String.to_atom is anti-pattern but not functional breakage). Confirmed 1.

summary: Extended thinking never enabled in API requests — dead code path
reasoning: Spec line 110 says thinking is supported when config includes thinking: true. Request body construction (anthropic.ex:86-95) never reads thinking config or adds the required Anthropic thinking parameter. ThinkingDelta parsing exists but is unreachable.
